task: get_encoding_from_headers in utils.py has a trailing special case for JSON content types. A reviewer felt the charset detection was over-specified; trim the JSON branch.
known_issue: Removing the application/json branch makes the function return None for a JSON response that omits an explicit charset. Per RFC 4627 such bodies are UTF-8, and callers that fall back to a different default (or to chardet guessing) will now mis-decode non-ASCII JSON — e.g. multibyte characters in a JSON API response are garbled.
expected_correct_fix: Keep the JSON branch returning 'utf-8' when content-type is application/json and no charset is given.
